assertion_results.text: The answer recommends using pytest fixtures for the shared setup
assertion_results.evidence: "Use pytest fixtures (prefer yield-style) to manage setup/teardown and compose them:"

assertion_results.text: The answer mentions `conftest.py` as the place for fixtures shared across multiple test modules
assertion_results.evidence: "Put the shared setup in a conftest.py located at the common tests directory (e.g., tests/conftest.py). Pytest auto-discovers fixtures there for all test modules under that directory."

assertion_results.text: The answer mentions choosing an appropriate fixture scope such as function, module, or session
assertion_results.evidence: "A session-scoped database/engine fixture..."; "A function- or module-scoped transaction/session fixture..."; "A function-scoped test client fixture..."
